req.title: Create patient
req.statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-001 mandates that a clinician can create a patient with valid demographics (positive path) and that creation is rejected with HTTP 422 when mandatory elements are absent (negative path). A boundary case exercises the minimum viable payload — exactly the mandatory fields and nothing more — confirming the system accepts precisely the threshold input. EP partitions valid vs. invalid payloads; NEG targets the explicit error pathway; BVA probes the exact minimum-field boundary. STC and DT are less applicable here because the requirement is stateless and single-rule, so EP, NEG, and BVA cover the required three heuristics cleanly.

tests.name: test_create_patient_with_full_valid_demographics
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a registered clinician (role:clinician)
fhir-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
No pre-existing patient with the same identifier exists
actions: Build a FHIR Patient payload with all mandatory and common optional fields
POST payload to /Patient with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a resource id
family name in response matches submitted value
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_with_full_valid_demographics(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_identifier):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:fhir-lite:mrn", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "name": [{"use": "official", "family": "Doe", "given": ["Jane"]}],
        "gender": "female",
        "birthDate": "1985-06-15"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert "id" in body and body["id"]
    names = body.get("name", [])
    assert any(n.get("family") == "Doe" for n in names)

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_mandatory_name_returns_422
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a registered clinician (role:clinician)
fhir-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a FHIR Patient payload that omits the mandatory 'name' element
POST payload to /Patient with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body describes the validation failure
trace: R-HLT-001: creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected
R-HLT-001: error pathway 422 when mandatory element missing
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_missing_mandatory_name_returns_422(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_identifier):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:fhir-lite:mrn", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "1990-01-01"
        # 'name' intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    # Confirm error detail references the missing field
    raw = str(body).lower()
    assert "name" in raw or "mandatory" in raw or "required" in raw

tests.name: test_create_patient_with_only_mandatory_fields_accepted
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a registered clinician (role:clinician)
fhir-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
Payload contains EXACTLY the mandatory fields — no optional fields
actions: Build a FHIR Patient payload containing only the minimum mandatory elements
POST payload to /Patient with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a resource id
No optional fields are required for success
trace: R-HLT-001: validated demographics — boundary at minimum mandatory set
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_with_only_mandatory_fields_accepted(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_identifier):
    # Boundary: exactly the mandatory fields, zero optional fields
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"family": "Smith"}]
        # identifier, gender, birthDate intentionally absent — not mandatory per FHIR R4 base
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at minimum boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert "id" in body and body["id"]

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_birth_date_returns_422
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a registered clinician (role:clinician)
fhir-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
fhir-lite profile requires birthDate as mandatory
actions: Build a FHIR Patient payload that includes name and gender but omits birthDate
POST payload to /Patient with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body references birthDate or mandatory validation failure
trace: R-HLT-001: creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected
R-HLT-001: error pathway 422 when mandatory element missing
DT rule: name=present, gender=present, birthDate=absent => reject
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_missing_birth_date_returns_422(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_identifier):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:fhir-lite:mrn", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "name": [{"use": "official", "family": "Brown", "given": ["Alice"]}],
        "gender": "female"
        # 'birthDate' intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    raw = str(resp.json()).lower()
    assert "birthdate" in raw or "birth" in raw or "required" in raw or "mandatory" in raw
